content.text: Treating prompts as code is a necessary precondition for reliable AI systems in production. A prompt is a specification: it determines model behavior, output format, and safety constraints. Without version control, teams cannot reproduce past behavior, diagnose regressions, or roll back when a change breaks production.
Every production prompt must be versioned, signed, and deployable as an immutable artifact — the same way you treat a Docker image or a compiled binary.

This sub-subtopic covers the full lifecycle: authoring prompts in Git, building them into signed artifacts, storing them in a registry, deploying with pinned references, and rolling back without a re-build.

attrs.alt: Prompt artifact version control lifecycle from authoring through deployment and rollback

content.text: A prompt artifact is more than a text file. It is a bundle that includes the prompt template, inference parameters, the model pin, and evaluation metadata — all hashed together so no part can change without invalidating the version.

# prompts/triage-v2/v1.2.0/prompt.yaml
version: "1.2.0"
model: claude-haiku-4-5-20251001
max_tokens: 2048
temperature: 0.3
system: |
  You are a support triage agent. Classify the user's issue into exactly one
  category: BILLING, TECHNICAL, ACCOUNT, or OTHER.
  Respond with JSON: {"category": "<CAT>", "confidence": <0-1>}.
user_template: |
  Issue: {{ issue_text }}
  Priority: {{ priority }}
template_vars:
  - issue_text
  - priority
eval_suite: evals/triage-v2.json
The file path itself encodes the version. The CI pipeline produces a SHA-256 hash of the entire bundle directory and stores it alongside the artifact — any mutation of any file breaks the hash.
A clear on-disk layout makes it easy to enumerate versions, compare diffs, and automate promotion:

prompts/
  triage-v2/                  # logical prompt name (never change)
    v1.0.0/
      prompt.yaml
      schema.json             # JSON schema for expected model output
      evals/                  # eval dataset symlinked or copied at tag time
    v1.1.0/
      prompt.yaml
      schema.json
    v1.2.0/
      prompt.yaml
      schema.json
    current -> v1.2.0         # symlink updated by CD; never used in code
  summariser/
    v2.0.0/
      prompt.yaml
Naming rules that prevent ambiguity in registries and deployment configs:
kebab-case, describes function, never changes across versions (
semver (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH). MAJOR = breaking change in output schema; MINOR = new capability; PATCH = wording fix that does not alter output structure.
always the full model ID in
Packaging and Signing Prompts in CI
The CI pipeline packages each versioned directory into a tarball, hashes it, and signs it with a project key before pushing to the registry. This prevents silent mutation in storage.

# .github/workflows/prompt-publish.yml
name: Publish Prompt Artifact

on:
  push:
    paths:
      - 'prompts/**'
    tags:
      - 'prompt/*'          # e.g. prompt/triage-v2/v1.2.0

jobs:
  publish:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      id-token: write        # for OIDC signing

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Extract prompt name and version from tag
        run: |
          TAG="${GITHUB_REF_NAME}"          # prompt/triage-v2/v1.2.0
          PROMPT=$(echo "$TAG" | cut -d/ -f2)
          VERSION=$(echo "$TAG" | cut -d/ -f3)
          echo "PROMPT=$PROMPT" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "VERSION=$VERSION" >> $GITHUB_ENV

      - name: Validate prompt.yaml schema
        run: |
          pip install -q jsonschema pyyaml
          python scripts/validate_prompt_yaml.py \
            prompts/$PROMPT/$VERSION/prompt.yaml

      - name: Run eval gate
        env:
          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          python scripts/run_evals.py \
            --prompt prompts/$PROMPT/$VERSION/prompt.yaml \
            --suite prompts/$PROMPT/$VERSION/evals/ \
            --min-score 0.90

      - name: Package and hash
        run: |
          tar czf $PROMPT-$VERSION.tar.gz prompts/$PROMPT/$VERSION/
          sha256sum $PROMPT-$VERSION.tar.gz > $PROMPT-$VERSION.sha256

      - name: Sign with cosign (keyless)
        uses: sigstore/cosign-installer@v3
      - run: |
          cosign sign-blob --yes \
            --output-signature $PROMPT-$VERSION.sig \
            $PROMPT-$VERSION.tar.gz

      - name: Push to S3 registry
        run: |
          aws s3 cp $PROMPT-$VERSION.tar.gz \
            s3://acme-prompt-registry/prompts/$PROMPT/$VERSION/
          aws s3 cp $PROMPT-$VERSION.sha256 \
            s3://acme-prompt-registry/prompts/$PROMPT/$VERSION/
          aws s3 cp $PROMPT-$VERSION.sig \
            s3://acme-prompt-registry/prompts/$PROMPT/$VERSION/
A schema validator catches missing fields, invalid model IDs, and out-of-range parameters before the artifact is ever published. This runs as the first step in CI, before any API calls.
Production services must never reference a floating version like
Production services must never reference a floating version like
. Instead, the deployment config pins an exact version and the deployment script verifies the hash before loading the prompt into memory.

# deploy/config/production.yaml
prompts:
  triage:
    name: triage-v2
    version: "1.2.0"
    sha256: "a3f9c2e7d841b..."    # from .sha256 file in registry
  summariser:
    name: summariser
    version: "2.0.0"
    sha256: "f12a3d9e..."

# src/prompt_loader.py
import hashlib, tarfile, yaml
from pathlib import Path
import boto3

REGISTRY_BUCKET = "acme-prompt-registry"

def load_prompt(name: str, version: str, expected_sha256: str) -> dict:
    key_prefix = f"prompts/{name}/{version}"
    tarball = f"/tmp/{name}-{version}.tar.gz"

    s3 = boto3.client("s3")
    s3.download_file(REGISTRY_BUCKET, f"{key_prefix}/{name}-{version}.tar.gz", tarball)

    actual = hashlib.sha256(Path(tarball).read_bytes()).hexdigest()
    if actual != expected_sha256:
        raise ValueError(
            f"Integrity check failed for {name}@{version}: "
            f"expected {expected_sha256}, got {actual}"
        )

    with tarfile.open(tarball) as tf:
        tf.extractall("/tmp/prompts/")

    return yaml.safe_load(
        Path(f"/tmp/prompts/prompts/{name}/{version}/prompt.yaml").read_text()
    )
The integrity check runs at service startup. A mismatched hash aborts startup — it does not log a warning and continue.
Rolling Back to a Previous Version

Because every artifact is immutable and pinned by hash, rollback is a configuration change — not a code change, not a re-build. The deployment config is updated to reference the previous version and the service restarts.

# Emergency rollback: revert triage-v2 to 1.1.0
# 1. Update the pinned version in the deployment config
sed -i 's/version: "1.2.0"/version: "1.1.0"/' deploy/config/production.yaml
sed -i 's/sha256: "a3f9c2e7d841b..."/sha256: "b9c4f1a2..."/' deploy/config/production.yaml

# 2. Commit and push (triggers CD pipeline)
git add deploy/config/production.yaml
git commit -m "hotfix: rollback triage-v2 to 1.1.0 (regression in PR classification)"
git push origin main

# 3. Verify the running version after deploy
curl -s https://api.example.com/internal/prompt-versions | jq .triage
# => { "name": "triage-v2", "version": "1.1.0", "sha256": "b9c4f1a2..." }
A version endpoint that reports the loaded prompt name, version, and hash makes rollback verification trivial without access to the deployment infrastructure.
Every API call in production should include the loaded prompt version in request metadata. This enables attribution of behavior changes to specific prompt releases.

# src/claude_client.py
import anthropic
from .prompt_loader import load_prompt
from .config import DEPLOY_CONFIG

client = anthropic.Anthropic()

def call_triage(issue_text: str, priority: str) -> dict:
    cfg = DEPLOY_CONFIG["prompts"]["triage"]
    prompt = load_prompt(cfg["name"], cfg["version"], cfg["sha256"])

    user_msg = prompt["user_template"].replace(
        "{{ issue_text }}", issue_text
    ).replace("{{ priority }}", priority)

    response = client.messages.create(
        model=prompt["model"],
        max_tokens=prompt["max_tokens"],
        temperature=prompt["temperature"],
        system=prompt["system"],
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": user_msg}],
    )

    return {
        "result": response.content[0].text,
        "prompt_version": f"{cfg['name']}@{cfg['version']}",
        "model": prompt["model"],
        "input_tokens": response.usage.input_tokens,
        "output_tokens": response.usage.output_tokens,
    }

field with every API call. In your observability stack, you can then slice latency, error rate, and output quality metrics by prompt version — this is what makes it possible to detect regressions that only manifest at scale.

Before promoting a new version, engineers compare the outputs of the candidate against the current production version on a shared eval set. This diff should be part of every pull request that changes a prompt.

# scripts/compare_prompt_versions.py
import json, anthropic, yaml
from pathlib import Path

client = anthropic.Anthropic()

def run_prompt(prompt_yaml_path: str, inputs: list[dict]) -> list[str]:
    cfg = yaml.safe_load(Path(prompt_yaml_path).read_text())
    results = []
    for inp in inputs:
        user_msg = cfg["user_template"]
        for k, v in inp.items():
            user_msg = user_msg.replace("{{ " + k + " }}", v)
        r = client.messages.create(
            model=cfg["model"],
            max_tokens=cfg["max_tokens"],
            temperature=0.0,          # deterministic for comparison
            system=cfg["system"],
            messages=[{"role": "user", "content": user_msg}],
        )
        results.append(r.content[0].text)
    return results

def compare(v_a: str, v_b: str, eval_inputs: list[dict]):
    name = "triage-v2"
    results_a = run_prompt(f"prompts/{name}/{v_a}/prompt.yaml", eval_inputs)
    results_b = run_prompt(f"prompts/{name}/{v_b}/prompt.yaml", eval_inputs)

    diffs = [(i, a, b) for i, (a, b) in enumerate(zip(results_a, results_b)) if a != b]
    print(f"Compared {len(eval_inputs)} inputs: {len(diffs)} differences")
    for idx, a, b in diffs:
        print(f"\n--- {v_a} input[{idx}] ---\n{a}")
        print(f"\n+++ {v_b} input[{idx}] ---\n{b}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    inputs = json.loads(Path("evals/triage-v2-inputs.json").read_text())
    compare("1.1.0", "1.2.0", inputs)
The Git tagging strategy must be enforced by branch protection rules so that only CI can publish artifacts:
Branch protection settings on the repository:
Require PR review from a prompt engineer before merging to main
Require the CI eval gate to pass (status check: prompt-eval)
Do not allow direct pushes to main — all changes via PR
Require signed commits (GPG or SSH signing)
Artifact registries grow without a retention policy. The cleanup script below deletes artifacts that are both old and no longer pinned in any deployment config:

# scripts/registry_cleanup.py
import boto3, yaml, re
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
from pathlib import Path

BUCKET = "acme-prompt-registry"
RETAIN_DAYS = 90
DEPLOY_CONFIGS = list(Path("deploy/config").glob("*.yaml"))

def pinned_versions() -> set[str]:
    pins = set()
    for cfg_path in DEPLOY_CONFIGS:
        cfg = yaml.safe_load(cfg_path.read_text())
        for info in cfg.get("prompts", {}).values():
            pins.add(f"{info['name']}/{info['version']}")
    return pins

def cleanup_old_artifacts():
    s3 = boto3.client("s3")
    pinned = pinned_versions()
    cutoff = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(days=RETAIN_DAYS)

    paginator = s3.get_paginator("list_objects_v2")
    for page in paginator.paginate(Bucket=BUCKET, Prefix="prompts/"):
        for obj in page.get("Contents", []):
            key = obj["Key"]
            # key: prompts/triage-v2/v1.0.0/...
            m = re.match(r"prompts/([^/]+)/([^/]+)/", key)
            if not m: continue
            version_key = f"{m.group(1)}/{m.group(2)}"
            if version_key in pinned: continue
            if obj["LastModified"] > cutoff: continue

            print(f"DELETE {key}")
            s3.delete_object(Bucket=BUCKET, Key=key)

cleanup_old_artifacts()
bundle prompt.yaml, schema, and eval suite together; hash the whole bundle.
MAJOR for breaking output schema changes, MINOR for new capabilities, PATCH for wording fixes.
validate schema, run eval suite with a minimum score threshold, sign before publishing to registry.
every production config pins exact version + SHA-256; hash verified at service startup.
revert the pinned version in deploy config and push — no re-build, no code change.
log prompt_version field on every API call to enable per-version metric slicing.
run candidate vs. current on shared eval inputs as part of every PR review.
retain artifacts for 90 days minimum; never delete a version still pinned in any deploy config.
